mechanism_narrative: Rising federal funds rates increase the discount rate applied to future equity cash flows, directly depressing S&P 500 forward returns over the next month.
failure_condition: 20-day forward returns show positive correlation with FEDFUNDS increases over any 24-month rolling window, or Rank IC > 0.05.

mechanism_narrative: When the unemployment rate breaches the 5.0 percent threshold, labor market slack triggers immediate profit margin compression and equity sell-offs within five trading days.
failure_condition: SPX produces positive 5-day returns in > 65% of instances where UNRATE crosses 5.0%, or the signal loses predictive power (Rank IC < 0.02) in walk-forward tests.

mechanism_narrative: Simultaneous expansion in retail sales and housing starts amplifies consumer spending and construction activity, generating sustained earnings growth that lifts S&P 500 returns over a quarter.
failure_condition: The joint signal fails to predict positive 60-day returns when RSAFS and HOUST both rise, or interaction term Rank IC remains below 0.03 across all test periods.

mechanism_narrative: Accelerating industrial production indicates rising capacity utilization and corporate output, which translates to higher equity valuations over the next month during expansionary phases.
failure_condition: INDPRO growth shows no statistically significant correlation with 20-day forward returns during recovery regimes, or the signal reverses direction (predicts negative returns) in > 50% of cases.

mechanism_narrative: A month-over-month CPI increase exceeding 0.4 percent triggers immediate market repricing of inflation expectations, forcing rapid equity liquidations within five trading days.
failure_condition: 5-day forward returns remain positive in > 60% of months where CPIAUCSL MoM change exceeds 0.4%, or the threshold effect vanishes in out-of-sample validation.

mechanism_narrative: Consistent monthly payroll employment growth sustains household income and aggregate demand, providing a reliable positive drift for S&P 500 returns over a three-month horizon.
failure_condition: PAYEMS growth shows negative or zero correlation with 60-day forward returns over any 36-month rolling window, or Rank IC < 0.02.

mechanism_narrative: Divergence between quarterly GDP growth and monthly retail sales indicates inventory accumulation and weakening consumer demand, triggering a negative equity adjustment over the next month.
failure_condition: The divergence signal predicts positive 20-day returns in > 55% of cases, or the interaction effect fails to achieve statistical significance (p > 0.10) in multivariate regression tests.

mechanism_narrative: High industrial production levels above 115.0 indicate full capacity utilization, which constrains future output growth and triggers short-term equity pullbacks within five trading days.
failure_condition: INDPRO levels above 115.0 consistently predict positive 5-day returns, or the signal loses predictive accuracy (Rank IC < 0.03) during peak-cycle periods.
